title: get_note_context
description: Fetch full note bodies plus parsed frontmatter for a small set of paths. Use during the query loop when reranked chunks are not enough and Gemma needs the surrounding note in full to answer correctly (e.g. step-by-step procedures, full quoted blocks, tables that were chunked). Do not use this as a substitute for search_notes — it does not search, it only reads. Result shape (not modeled here for v1): an array of {path, title, frontmatter, body_markdown, chunks?: [...]}, in the same order as the input paths.

items.description: Vault-relative path to a Markdown note in the index. Must not contain '..' segments.

paths.description: Vault-relative note paths to fetch. Keep this list short (<= 16) — full bodies are token-heavy and large lists blow the context budget. Order matters: the result list mirrors the input order so you can correlate them in subsequent prompts.
